mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates accelerating inflation, which forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >55% of months when CPIAUCSL increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in UNRATE exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered months or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST indicates robust consumer spending and housing market strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean return over 60 days in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power in baseline regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO during a late-cycle expansion reflects weakening industrial output that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in >55% of late-cycle months or shows no directional bias when filtered by business cycle phase.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in FEDFUNDS below 0.25% triggers liquidity-driven momentum buying as markets anticipate aggressive central bank support, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of triggered instances or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for prior market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE indicates labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative or flat 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 during a post-shock recovery phase signals fragile aggregate demand that undermines equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of post-shock quarters with negative GDPC1 growth, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in INDPRO reflect strengthening production capacity that supports near-term earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with rising INDPRO or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.02 across rolling windows.
